nodes.summary: Audited the WAH4P repository and saved a clear project snapshot for future work. The repo is split into a NestJS backend, a Flutter frontend, and supporting SQL/docs. The backend is a secured API with global API-key protection, throttling, strict env validation, Supabase auth/data integration, and Resend email delivery. The frontend is a Flutter mobile app with centralized theming, secure local storage, a startup gate, and MPIN/session lifecycle handling. The docs describe a Supabase/Postgres source of truth for patient data, with frontend screens mostly acting as presentation and interaction layers. I also captured the current testing surface and a couple of documentation mismatches that future work should reconcile.

nodes.summary: Saved a deeper project map for WAH4P that focuses on navigation, feature boundaries, and service responsibilities. The repository presents a clear two-tier architecture: a NestJS backend that owns auth/security/business rules and a Flutter frontend that owns display, navigation, and device-scoped state. The frontend route tree now has a precise map from startup through onboarding, auth, dashboard, PHR, EHR-style records, appointments, calendar, notifications, profile, and legal screens. The backend API surface is also clearer: a public health check plus a broad auth/profile domain that covers registration, login, 2FA, MPIN, password reset, and profile hydration/update. This memory layer is intended to function as an app map, not just a summary.

affectedFiles.reason: Defines the Flutter app shell, theme, route entrypoint, and resume-time lock behavior, which are central to the app map.
affectedFiles.notes: Confirms where app-wide navigation and lifecycle control live.

affectedFiles.reason: Contains the full named route registry and screen-to-route mapping for the Flutter client.
affectedFiles.notes: This is the best source of truth for the app’s visible navigation tree.

affectedFiles.notes: Useful for maintaining route names and screen grouping.

affectedFiles.reason: Shows how the app waits for startup resolution and then redirects to the correct initial route.
affectedFiles.notes: Important for understanding the launch flow.

affectedFiles.reason: Implements startup routing decisions from auth, onboarding, and MPIN state.
affectedFiles.notes: This is the decision engine behind splash-to-app routing.

affectedFiles.reason: Implements the app’s background timing and unlock requirement policy.
affectedFiles.notes: Key to understanding resume/lock behavior.

affectedFiles.reason: Directory structure reveals the major presentation feature families that make up the client.
affectedFiles.notes: Confirms the app is organized by domain feature.

affectedFiles.reason: Defines backend module composition and global guards, which frame the server-side architecture map.
affectedFiles.notes: Shows the core backend wiring.

affectedFiles.reason: Shows how the backend auth domain is composed from controllers, services, and repositories.
affectedFiles.notes: Critical for understanding auth responsibility split.

affectedFiles.reason: Lists the auth workflow endpoints and groups them into registration, login, 2FA, security, and MPIN routes.
affectedFiles.notes: Primary backend workflow surface.

affectedFiles.reason: Exposes profile hydration and update endpoints separate from the auth workflow controller.
affectedFiles.notes: Important for profile screen integration.

affectedFiles.reason: Provides the public health endpoint and marks the small public server surface.
affectedFiles.notes: Useful for operational checks.

nodes.summary: Added a plain-English atlas for WAH4P so future work can read the project like a map. The repo is best understood in layers: a Flutter client that controls navigation and presentation, a NestJS backend that owns security and business rules, and Supabase/Postgres plus secure local storage for persistence. The most important user journeys now have an explicit flow: splash/startup -> onboarding or login -> dashboard or MPIN unlock -> feature areas like records, appointments, resupply, calendar, notifications, profile, and legal screens. This memory also clarifies which screens are static, which are route-driven, and which backend APIs support each major journey.

nodes.summary: Fixed the Security Settings screen crash caused by calling ScaffoldMessenger.of(context) during initState-driven async hydration. The login redirect is now deferred with a post-frame callback, which keeps the cached preloaded Security screen behavior intact and avoids inherited-widget timing errors. Flutter analysis passed after the fix.

affectedFiles.reason: Changed the login redirect to run after the first frame so the screen no longer throws when initState-triggered async work calls ScaffoldMessenger too early.
affectedFiles.notes: The rest of the cache-first loading behavior remains intact.

nodes.summary: Reviewed the frontend Health Records screens and the existing Personal Records architecture to plan a future local persistence layer for Health Records. The current Health Records feature is still presentation-driven with hardcoded entry data in the screen files, while Personal Records already has a dedicated typed data/repository pattern. The recommended approach is to introduce a separate local health-records repository and domain model layer so the UI can read from a single source of truth now and later support creation without reworking the screens.

nodes.summary: Implemented read-only Supabase-backed Health Records storage integration. Added SQL schema for a single health_records table designed for manual Supabase inserts and future backend create endpoints, added backend database typings and a read-only NestJS HealthRecords module, and refactored Flutter Health Records screens to load authenticated records from the backend without adding any create/receive endpoints. Validation passed with backend npm run build and frontend flutter analyze.

nodes.summary: Implemented read-only Supabase-backed Health Records integration with one dedicated table per Health Records screen/category. Added SQL schema for medical_history_records, immunization_records, medical_consultation_records, and laboratory_result_records; added typed backend read-only module/endpoints; added Flutter API client, mapper, and data-loading wrapper; converted Health Records screens from embedded static entries to authenticated backend-loaded records. No create/receive endpoints were added, matching the user's request to handle creation later. Validation passed with backend npm run build and frontend flutter analyze.

nodes.summary: Removed PhilSys ID from the backend profile contract and aligned the frontend shared profile model with the trimmed response shape. The backend now no longer validates, stores, returns, or includes philSysId in auth/profile flows, and a SQL migration was added to strip existing philSysId keys from profiles.patient_profile JSONB rows. Backend build and targeted frontend Flutter tests passed after the change.

nodes.summary: Implemented Phase 2 of the interoperability rollout across backend and Flutter. The backend now exposes a WAH4PC-backed interoperability proxy for provider discovery and sync-request preparation, with env validation and e2e coverage. The Flutter app now launches a dedicated sync wizard from the Personal Information screen, uses bottom-sheet selection for identifier/provider choice, and reuses the existing profile readiness rules to gate entry. Tests for the new interoperability models, wizard flow, and profile-to-wizard navigation all pass.

nodes.summary: Implemented field-format validation for the Personal Information profile flow on both frontend and backend. The frontend profile form now validates text-like fields, phone numbers, postal code, and PhilHealth/PhilSys IDs before save. The backend DTOs now enforce matching rules for names, phone numbers, postal code, and identifier formats while still allowing either PhilHealth or PhilSys to satisfy the identifier requirement. Focused backend and Flutter tests passed, and the Kanban card was moved to done.

nodes.summary: Fixed the blank Weekly Health Report graph on the dashboard. The root cause was the sparkline painter in DashboardMetricCard returning early when a metric had only one trend point, which is common for real users with sparse recent BMI, blood pressure, or temperature data. The painter now renders a visible single-point marker, and a focused widget test covers the single-point case. The dashboard dashboard test slice passes again.

nodes.summary: Implemented the first-pass inbound WAH4PC/FHIR sync flow for wah4patients. The backend now has a dedicated fhir-sync module for provider-facing webhooks and practitioner discovery, routes are excluded from the global /api/v1 prefix where needed, patient identifier matching is backed by a new Supabase table, and inbound resources are normalized into the existing internal clinical tables instead of storing raw payloads. Validation and tests passed after the changes.

nodes.summary: Implemented the real outbound interoperability sync submission flow. The backend now sends sync requests to the gateway's /api/v1/fhir/request/{resourceType} endpoint using the configured requester provider ID, selected target provider, and patient identifier, then returns the submitted transaction details. The sync prepare endpoint now responds with HTTP 202 and the tests (unit, build, and e2e) all pass.

affectedFiles.notes: Keeps the contract explicit and stable.

affectedFiles.notes: Aligns the endpoint status with asynchronous processing.

affectedFiles.notes: Covers the new outbound request behavior.

nodes.summary: Implemented a new interoperability simulation flow so the Flutter sync wizard can post real PH Core example data into the backend and store it against the signed-in account/profile. The backend now exposes a simulated sync endpoint that reuses the existing prepare flow for validation, loads checked-in PH Core fixtures, and forwards a Bundle into the existing FHIR receive-results persistence path. The frontend gained a neutral secondary Simulate request button under Prepare request, plus request/result plumbing and updated review copy. Validation passed with backend e2e coverage and Flutter analysis.

nodes.summary: Adjusted the sync simulation flow so the user-facing request no longer carries a profileId in the body. The frontend now sends the authenticated account ID in an internal x-user-id header, while the backend simulation endpoint resolves the profile context from that header and persists the generated PH Core bundle through the existing receive-results-for-profile path. This preserves UUID-based internal storage without exposing profileId to the user and resolved the validation error the user was seeing.

affectedFiles.notes: Keeps account context internal to the transport layer.

affectedFiles.notes: The body now matches the user-facing request shape.

affectedFiles.notes: The button stays hidden from UUID details.

affectedFiles.notes: Verifies the new transport shape.

nodes.summary: Fixed the sync simulation auth-context problem by switching the frontend to send the authenticated Supabase access token as a Bearer Authorization header, and by making the backend simulation route resolve the current user from that token through AuthSupportService, with x-user-id only as a fallback. The request body no longer carries profileId, and the simulation still persists the PH Core bundle against the authenticated account via the existing receive-results-for-profile path. Validation passed after the change.

affectedFiles.notes: Keeps auth resolution reusable rather than duplicating it.

affectedFiles.notes: The route now supports bearer-token auth context.

affectedFiles.reason: Resolved profile ID from the bearer token using AuthSupportService, with x-user-id fallback, before persisting the simulated PH Core bundle.
affectedFiles.notes: This is the core runtime fix for the missing account context error.

affectedFiles.reason: Removed profileId from the public simulation DTO so the body only contains request data relevant to the sync action.
affectedFiles.notes: Prevents users from having to provide internal UUIDs.

affectedFiles.reason: Removed the obsolete public simulation payload type that exposed profileId.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps the contract aligned with the new auth-based flow.

affectedFiles.reason: Updated the simulation e2e test to use a bearer Authorization header and mocked AuthSupportService account resolution.
affectedFiles.notes: Verifies the real auth-context path instead of the fallback header.

affectedFiles.notes: Matches the rest of the app’s authenticated API calls.

nodes.summary: Fixed the sync simulation failure that surfaced as 'unable to store observation record' by making the backend observation/lab record filter text length-safe before insert. The root cause was an overlong laboratory_result_records.filter_value exceeding the database's 80-character check constraint. Added a reusable filter builder in the FHIR sync mapper so clinical record filter values are bounded, and added regression coverage for the PH Core parsers to assert filterValue length stays within 80 characters. Verified with targeted backend tests, backend build, and the sync simulation e2e route test.

affectedFiles.reason: Added the length-safe filter builder and updated clinical record mapping so inserted filter values cannot exceed the database constraint.
affectedFiles.notes: This is the primary production fix for the observation storage failure.

affectedFiles.reason: Added regression coverage asserting clinical record filter values stay within the 80-character database limit.
affectedFiles.notes: Covers the observation fixture path that previously failed.

nodes.summary: Fixed the sync simulation failure that surfaced as 'unable to store observation record' by making the backend observation/lab record filter text length-safe before insert. The root cause was an overlong laboratory_result_records.filter_value exceeding the database's 80-character check constraint. Added a reusable filter builder in the FHIR sync mapper so clinical record filter values are bounded, and added regression coverage for the PH Core parsers to assert filterValue length stays within 80 characters. Verified with targeted backend tests, backend build, and the sync simulation e2e route test.

nodes.summary: Relaxed personal information save behavior so the frontend and backend no longer require all fields for profile updates, while sync remains gated strictly by identifier presence. The backend now accepts partial profile drafts, merges them with existing profile data, and keeps the sync-lock enforcement introduced earlier. Frontend form fields were changed to optional validators and the sync button still depends on having at least one identifier. Regression tests were updated and targeted Flutter/Jest suites passed.

affectedFiles.notes: New targeted unit tests for the profile service.

affectedFiles.notes: Also updated button style assertions to WidgetState.

affectedFiles.notes: No longer depends on overall profile completeness.

nodes.summary: Standardized the PhilHealth identifier system URI across the sync flow to the PH Core canonical URI http://philhealth.gov.ph/fhir/Identifier/philhealth-id. The backend now canonicalizes legacy http://philhealth.gov.ph identifiers for lookup and storage compatibility, while the Flutter sync UI sends the new URI. Verified affected backend and frontend tests pass after the change.
